m8_note: SERVED M=8 WEIGHT-READ EFFICIENCY ~FLAT: marlin_m8_hbm_eff=0.256 vs M=1 0.258 (delta -0.002), < 0.5. This matches the Marlin prior (2408.11743 Tab2: int4 GEMM time ~flat M=1->8, speedup 2.93->2.90x): Marlin is ALREADY software-pipelined at M=1, so M=8 does more compute at ~flat weight-read time -> efficiency does NOT rise. M=8 IS bandwidth-bound as a REGIME (M<~64 crossover, Fig 11), but that does NOT add efficiency-vs-peak headroom. Strict measured-floor lift +15.7 TPS (M=1 was +15.8) does NOT clear #383 +23.75 robust -> the supply lift does NOT improve at the served width; the strict-tier STRADDLE STANDS. HONEST NEGATIVE (#388 discipline): the served regime does not upgrade the M=1 straddle to a pass.
realized_note: ROOFLINE BOUND: no cb3/QTIP kernel in env (Marlin/AWQ/AQLM only); cb3 bounded by its nominal 0.785x byte count at int4-Marlin's measured M=1 BW-efficiency. Tight iff cb3 matches that efficiency -- literature-supported (QTIP/QuIP# L1-resident codebook, RHT folded offline; only a <<1% online activation Hadamard). Measured count-weighted Marlin M=1 BW-eff = 0.258 (peak-copy 470.6 GB/s); m1_is_bw_bound=False. NOT fully BW-bound at M=1 (launch/latency-influenced) => roofline is an OPTIMISTIC upper bound; literature-conservative 1.15x is the realized floor.
